Record Class Accumulated<E,T>

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
io.github.igorgf.control.Accumulated<E,T>
Type Parameters:
E - The contained error type.
T - The validation target type.
Record Components:
errors - The contained list of accumulated errors E. Never null or empty.
All Implemented Interfaces:
Invalid<E,T>, Validation<E,T>
record Accumulated<E,T>(List<E> errors) extends Record implements Invalid<E,T>
The invalid, left side, implementation of Validation<E, T>, inheriting shared failed validation behavior from Invalid<E, T>.

Contains an accumulation of validation errors in a List<E>, preserving errors of any Accumulated passed into Validation.combine(Validation) or its overloads.

See Also:
  • Validation
  • Invalid
  • Critical
  • Valid

  • Constructor Details

    • Accumulated

      Accumulated(List<E> errors)
      Creates an instance of a Accumulated record class.
      Parameters:
      errors - the value for the errors record component

  • Method Details

    • combineInvalid

      public <S,U> Invalid<E,U> combineInvalid(Invalid<E,S> other)
      The applicative functor operation of Invalid. Similar to Invalid.combine(Validation). Used to explicitly combine two Invalid validations.

      Scenarios:

      • When this is a Accumulated and other is Accumulated, combines errors from this and other into a new Accumulated<E, U>. Preserving the contained errors, and binding to the new target type U.
      • When this is a Accumulated and other is Critical, returns other as Invalid<E, U>. Preserving the contained error, and binding the new target type U.
      • When this is a Critical, ignores other, returns this as Invalid<E, U>. Preserving the contained error, and binding the new target type U.

      The unchecked cast from other to Invalid<E, U> is provably safe because a Critical does not contain any value S, it only contains error E.

      Specified by:
      combineInvalid in interface Invalid<E,T>
      Type Parameters:
      S - The type of object validated by other.
      U - The validation target type to bind going forward.
      Parameters:
      other - Invalid to combine with this.
      Returns:
      Invalid<E, U> based on scenarios outlined above.
      See Also:
      • Invalid.combine(Validation)
      • combineInvalid(Invalid)
      • Critical.combineInvalid(Invalid)

    • mapError

      public <U, X extends Exception> Validation<U,T> mapError(CheckedFunction<? super E, ? extends U, ? extends X> errorMapper) throws X
      Allows item by item conversion of any contained errors from type E to type U, when this is Invalid. When this is Valid the conversion is not applied as there are no errors, but the new error type binding U is preserved.

      The errorMapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null or empty errors as errors() and Critical.error() can never be null or empty.

      Checked exceptions thrown by errorMapper are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      mapError in interface Validation<E,T>
      Type Parameters:
      U - The new error type.
      Parameters:
      errorMapper - Used to map errors from type E to type U.
      Returns:
      New Accumulated<U, T>, accumulating the results of errorMapper applied to each errors element.
      Throws:
      X - If the errorMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If errorMapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.mapError(CheckedFunction)
      • mapError(CheckedFunction)
      • Critical.mapError(CheckedFunction)

    • foldErrors

      public <U, X extends Exception> Validation<U,T> foldErrors(CheckedFunction<? super List<E>, ? extends U, ? extends X> errorMapper) throws X
      Allows collapsing contained errors into a new type U, when this is Invalid. When this is Valid the fold is not applied as there are no errors, but the new error type binding U is preserved.

      The errorMapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null or empty errors as errors() and Critical.error() can never be null or empty.

      Checked exceptions thrown by errorMapper are propagated, see Validation class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      foldErrors in interface Validation<E,T>
      Type Parameters:
      U - The new error type.
      Parameters:
      errorMapper - Used to collapse errors into a new type U.
      Returns:
      New Accumulated<U, T> containing the result of errorMapper.
      Throws:
      X - If the errorMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If errorMapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Valid.foldErrors(CheckedFunction)
      • foldErrors(CheckedFunction)
      • Critical.foldErrors(CheckedFunction)

    • isAccumulated

      public boolean isAccumulated()
      Is this a Accumulated.
      Specified by:
      isAccumulated in interface Validation<E,T>
      Returns:
      true
      See Also:
      • Validation.isValid()
      • Validation.isInvalid()
      • Validation.isCritical()

    • isCritical

      public boolean isCritical()
      Is this a Critical.
      Specified by:
      isCritical in interface Validation<E,T>
      Returns:
      false
      See Also:
      • Validation.isValid()
      • Validation.isInvalid()
      • Validation.isAccumulated()

    • getErrors

      public List<E> getErrors()
      Projects this into a List<E>.

      When this is a Invalid, the result is a new List<E> preserving the contained errors. Otherwise, returns an empty List<E>.

      Specified by:
      getErrors in interface Validation<E,T>
      Returns:
      errors.
      See Also:
      • Validation.get()

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.

    • errors

      public List<E> errors()
      Returns the value of the errors record component.
      Returns:
      the value of the errors record component
